utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 appear. Now check if each is fully specified with required details: (1) health_coverage: required 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'. Plan provides two plan options with benefits and individual costs, but does not provide a single combined cost or clarify chosen plan; still, it does include costs for health plans, satisfying costs detail. (2) property_coverage: required 'home/contents insurance with costs'. Provided plan name, benefits, and a cost (95) — fully specified. (3) travel_coverage: required 'travel insurance with costs'. Provided plan name, benefits, and a cost (110) — fully specified. (4) additional_services: required 'any enhancement services with costs'. Provided notes but no costs — missing required costs. (5) total_budget: required 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'. Provided monthly 405 and annual 4860 — fully specified. Thus, 4 of 5 items are fully specified; additional_services lacks costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four named items: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health), EliteCare Premium Plan (health), EliteHome Premium Coverage (property), and EliteTravel Premium Plan (travel). I located each of these in the ground-truth ratings under the corresponding categories. Their ratings are: LifeFirst Standard Plan (primary physical health) = 9; EliteCare Premium Plan (primary physical health) = 6; EliteHome Premium Coverage (home content/property protection) = 6; EliteTravel Premium Plan (travel insurance) = 5.
